description: Seed corpus of foundational black-hole research sources. Each entry generates a source-card markdown file under docs/research/source_cards/.

sources.title: Uber das Gravitationsfeld eines Massenpunktes nach der Einsteinschen Theorie
sources.venue: Sitzungsberichte der Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften
sources.summary: First exact non-trivial solution to Einstein's vacuum field equations. Describes the spacetime geometry outside a non-rotating, uncharged, spherically symmetric mass.

claims.statement: Outside a spherically symmetric mass M, in vacuum, the metric takes the Schwarzschild form.
The Schwarzschild radius is r_s = 2GM/c^2.

sources.title: Gravitational field of a spinning mass as an example of algebraically special metrics
sources.summary: Exact axially symmetric vacuum solution to Einstein's field equations describing rotating black holes.

claims.statement: The Kerr metric in Boyer-Lindquist coordinates depends on mass M and angular momentum per unit mass a.

sources.title: Rotating black holes: Locally nonrotating frames, energy extraction, and scalar synchrotron radiation
sources.summary: Definitive analytic treatment of equatorial geodesics in Kerr. Source of the closed-form expressions for ISCO and photon-sphere radii.

claims.statement: Prograde Kerr ISCO in geometric units satisfies r_ISCO = 3 + Z2 - sign(a)*sqrt((3 - Z1)(3 + Z1 + 2 Z2)).
Schwarzschild limit a -> 0 gives r_ISCO = 6.0 exactly.

sources.title: Gravitation
sources.summary: Standard graduate textbook on general relativity. Comprehensive reference for the analytic formulas used throughout the project.

claims.statement: Metric signature convention (-+++) and Schwarzschild metric in standard form.

sources.title: The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes
sources.summary: Definitive monograph on perturbation theory of black holes (Schwarzschild, Reissner-Nordstrom, Kerr).

claims.statement: Quasi-normal mode equations for Schwarzschild and Kerr perturbations.

sources.title: Gravitational collapse and space-time singularities
sources.summary: Proved that, under physically reasonable energy conditions, gravitational collapse must lead to a spacetime singularity.

claims.statement: Trapped-surface formation implies geodesic incompleteness.

sources.title: The four laws of black hole mechanics
sources.summary: Formal analogy between black-hole mechanics and the laws of thermodynamics: zeroth law (constant surface gravity), first law (mass-area-angular-momentum-charge relation), second law (area non-decrease), third law (extremality limit).

claims.statement: Black-hole surface gravity is constant over the horizon (zeroth law).

sources.title: Black holes and entropy
sources.summary: Proposed that black holes carry entropy proportional to their horizon area.

claims.statement: Black-hole entropy is S = (k_B c^3 / 4 hbar G) A.

sources.title: Black hole explosions?
sources.summary: Predicted that black holes emit thermal radiation due to quantum effects near the event horizon. Predicted but unobserved.

claims.statement: Hawking temperature T = hbar c^3 / (8 pi G M k_B).

sources.notes: Tier is speculative_extension because Hawking radiation, while theoretically well-established, has never been directly observed. Modules implementing related quantities must inherit this tier.

sources.title: On the proof of the positive mass conjecture in general relativity
sources.summary: Proved that the ADM mass of an asymptotically flat spacetime obeying the dominant energy condition is non-negative, with equality iff the spacetime is Minkowski.

claims.statement: ADM mass >= 0 for asymptotically flat spacetimes obeying the dominant energy condition.

sources.title: Observation of gravitational waves from a binary black hole merger
sources.summary: First direct detection of gravitational waves, from the merger of two stellar-mass black holes at z ~ 0.09.

claims.statement: Initial component masses: 36.2 +5.2/-3.8 and 29.1 +3.7/-4.4 solar masses (source frame).
Final remnant mass approximately 62.3 +3.7/-3.1 solar masses.

sources.title: First M87 Event Horizon Telescope results. I. The shadow of the supermassive black hole
sources.summary: First horizon-scale image of a supermassive black hole, M87*. Observed an asymmetric emission ring with reported diameter 42 +/- 3 microarcseconds, consistent with the shadow predicted by Kerr black holes in general relativity.

claims.statement: M87* ring diameter is 42 +/- 3 microarcseconds.
M87* mass approximately 6.5 +/- 0.7 x 10^9 solar masses.

sources.title: First Sagittarius A* Event Horizon Telescope results. I. The shadow of the supermassive black hole in the center of the Milky Way
sources.summary: First horizon-scale image of Sgr A*, the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way. Ring diameter 51.8 +/- 2.3 microarcseconds, consistent with prior mass measurements from stellar orbits.

claims.statement: Sgr A* ring diameter is 51.8 +/- 2.3 microarcseconds.

sources.title: Measuring distance and properties of the Milky Way's central supermassive black hole
sources.summary: Measured the mass of Sgr A* to approximately 4.1 x 10^6 solar masses via tracking stellar orbits at the galactic center.

claims.statement: Sgr A* mass approximately 4.1 +/- 0.6 x 10^6 solar masses.

sources.title: Theoreme d'existence pour certains systemes d'equations aux derivees partielles non lineaires
sources.summary: Proved that the Einstein equations admit a well-posed initial-value formulation in harmonic coordinates. Foundation of numerical relativity.

claims.statement: Einstein's vacuum equations form a well-posed quasilinear hyperbolic system in harmonic coordinates.

sources.title: An approach to gravitational radiation by a method of spin coefficients
sources.summary: Tetrad-based formulation of general relativity using null vectors and complex spin coefficients. Well-suited for studying gravitational radiation and algebraically special spacetimes.

claims.statement: Petrov-type-D spacetimes (such as Kerr) admit simplified equations in the Newman-Penrose formalism.

sources.title: GWTC-4.0: Updates to the gravitational-wave transient catalog
sources.summary: GWTC-4.0 adds 128 new compact-binary candidates from O4a, bringing the combined catalog to 218 candidates meeting the stated p_astro >= 0.5 and not-vetoed criteria.

claims.statement: GWTC-4.0 contains 218 compact-binary candidates above the stated significance threshold.

sources.title: Gravitational collapse: the role of general relativity
sources.summary: Introduced the energy-extraction process by which a particle entering the ergosphere of a rotating black hole can split such that one fragment falls in and the other escapes with more energy than the original.

claims.statement: Energy can be extracted from the rotational energy of a Kerr black hole via the Penrose process.

sources.title: Microscopic origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
sources.summary: Counted the microstates of certain extremal black holes in Type II string theory and reproduced the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy. Important conceptual result, but assumes string theory.

claims.statement: Counting BPS-state microstates of specific extremal black holes reproduces S = A / 4 to leading order.

sources.notes: Tier is speculative_extension because the result is framework-dependent (requires string theory).

sources.title: Disquisitiones generales circa superficies curvas
sources.venue: Commentationes Soc. Reg. Sci. Gott. Recent.
sources.summary: Established intrinsic differential geometry of surfaces and the Theorema Egregium: Gaussian curvature is an intrinsic invariant independent of embedding.

claims.statement: Gaussian curvature of a surface is intrinsic.
